title: Satires III: Against the City of Rome
description: Juvenal's friend Umbricius flees Rome: the city is too corrupt, too crowded, too Greek, too dangerous to live in.
introduction: Juvenal's friend Umbricius flees Rome: the city is too corrupt, too crowded, too Greek, too dangerous to live in.

translation.text: What can I do in Rome? I never learned to lie.
